Program_Description: Nurse Home Visiting Programs provide support to first-time, low-income pregnant women through home visits.
The Maternal Child Adolescent Health Program (MCAH) aims to improve the health and well-being of women and birthing persons.
Target_Population: Infants, children, adolescents, women, and birthing persons

Program_Description: Medi-Cal provides health coverage for low-income individuals, including pregnant women.
Target_Population: Pregnant individuals meeting income thresholds; varies by state expansion status

Program_Description: Parents as Teachers provides support and education for expectant parents and families with young children.
Target_Population: Expectant parents and families with children from birth through age 3 (or 5 in some cases)

Program_Description: Public Health Nurses provide bereavement support, information, resources, and follow-up services to assist families and caregivers who have experienced the loss of an infant from SIDS or Sudden Unexpected Infant Death (SUID).
Target_Population: Families and caregivers who have experienced the loss of an infant.

Program_Description: Nurse Home Visiting Programs provide support and resources for families during pregnancy and after childbirth.
Target_Population: First-time, low-income pregnant women enrolled by 28 weeks gestation

Program_Description: The WIC program provides nutritional support and education to low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women, as well as infants and children.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children

Program_Description: Nurse home visiting programs provide support and education to first-time, low-income pregnant women through home visits by registered nurses.
Target_Population: First-time, low-income pregnant women enrolled by 28 weeks gestation

Target_Population: Pregnant individuals meeting income thresholds; varies by state expansion status
